data_note: The 'human' field in cand6_956.json is mismatched: it reviews Apache Fluo cache-config code (FluoConfigurationImpl.java), not Drifty PR #956. Per prior round, the real human review for this PR was a bare approval with no findings. Missed-baseline therefore = my own verified findings.

own_review.issue: Fetch URL targets SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt — the MAIN repo's master, where disclaimer.txt does not exist until this PR merges (verified absent at base commit). Until merge, every CI/preview build (including the linked Vercel preview) renders GitHub's raw '404: Not Found' body inside the <pre> as the disclaimer. Chicken-and-egg; the PR description's 'loaded from my fork' note is stale — the diff already points at the main repo.

No error handling: no response.ok check, no try/catch, no fallback UI. A raw.githubusercontent outage throws during prerender; a 404 silently renders the error body as legal text. Same flaw exists in privacy-policy/terms pages, but this is new code; the in-repo download page (try/catch l.37, ok-check l.41, Retry l.102) shows the better pattern.

master-branch URL is mutable; sibling privacy-policy page pins a commit SHA (c86ce857...). Better still: disclaimer.txt lives in the same repo — read it locally at build time and drop the network dependency.
Disclaimer text duplicated in README.md and disclaimer.txt; copies will drift.
Unrelated, undocumented styling change: ul flex-1 -> flex-auto and space-x-6 -> space-x-4 alters footer column balance site-wide (copyright and social columns remain flex-1).
Unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit 0.3.1 -> 0.3.4 bump (plus nested @eslint/core 0.15.1) with no package.json change — scope creep (incidentally a dev-only ReDoS fix).

points.point: page.tsx:4 claims the fetch URL 'points to the author's fork' and should be updated
points.note: False: SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty is the main repo (PR author is AvinashAbbigeri). v5 echoed the stale PR-description note instead of verifying the diff, which already contains the main-repo URL. Counted as an absence-fabrication.

points.point: page.tsx:4 recommend commit-pinned URL like privacy-policy (c86ce857); notes terms page also unpinned/fragile
points.note: Verified: privacy-policy pins a SHA; terms fetches master/LICENSE unpinned.

points.point: page.tsx:1-15 no error handling (no response.ok / try/catch / fallback); matches existing pages; suggests guard across all three legal pages
points.note: Verified. 'Crash the build' is overstated for a 404 (text() succeeds, error body rendered) but correct for network failures during prerender.

points.point: README vs disclaimer.txt duplication, drift risk, suggests linking /disclaimer instead
package-lock.json unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit bump, recommend reverting for scope
points.note: Verified: no package.json change in the PR.
Raises no actionable concern and omits the flex-1 -> flex-auto behavioral half of the change.

missed.finding: disclaimer.txt does not exist on main-repo master at base, so pre-merge preview/CI builds render '404: Not Found' as the disclaimer (own_review #1). v5's pin-before-merge remediation is adjacent but it never states the file is absent today — and its 'fork' misread shows it did not actually check.

points.point: page.tsx:3-4 no error handling on fetch; cites in-repo download/page.tsx pattern (try/catch, response.ok, error UI with Retry) as the remediation; suggests retrofitting privacy-policy/terms
points.note: Verified: download/page.tsx has try/catch (l.37), ok-check (l.41), Retry button (l.102). 'Crashes at build/SSG time' on a 404 is slightly overstated (404 body renders silently), correct for network failure.

points.point: page.tsx:3 PR description contradicts the code: URL already points to main repo SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty, not a fork; asks to clarify which is correct
points.note: Verified and exactly right — this is the discrepancy v5 turned into a fabrication. v6 read the diff instead of parroting the stale description.

points.point: README vs disclaimer.txt duplicated content, drift risk, consolidate to single source of truth
Footer.tsx:16 space-x-6 -> space-x-4 incidental change; call out if intentional, revert if not
points.note: Accurate and actionable, though it omits the flex-1 -> flex-auto half of the same line's change.

missed.finding: disclaimer.txt does not exist on main-repo master at base, so pre-merge preview/CI builds render '404: Not Found' as the disclaimer (own_review #1). v6 names 404 as a hypothetical failure mode and even inspected the URL/fork question, but never states the file is absent on master today.

exceeds: Catching that the PR description's 'loaded from my fork' note is stale against the actual diff — the exact trap the other candidate fell into; verified correct.
Citing the in-repo download page (try/catch + response.ok + Retry UI) as a concrete, verified remediation pattern beyond anything in the human reference.

qualitative: The supplied human reference is mismatched (an Apache Fluo review), so both candidates were judged against my own verified findings; the real human review from prior rounds was a bare approval. v6 is the stronger review: it correctly identified that the fetch URL already points to the main repo and flagged the stale PR description, precisely where v5 committed its one absence-fabrication by asserting the URL 'points to the author's fork'. v6 also grounded its error-handling point in the repo's own download-page pattern (verified: try/catch, ok-check, Retry button) and made its footer scope-creep point actionable, though it missed the unrelated package-lock churn that v5 caught. Neither candidate landed the sharpest finding — disclaimer.txt is absent from master at base, so every pre-merge build renders GitHub's raw '404: Not Found' as the legal disclaimer — and both were charged one miss for it (applied symmetrically this round, unlike round 5's leniency toward v5). Net: v5 +2, v6 +3; v6 wins on factual discipline and verified depth, v5 on breadth of scope-creep coverage.
